title: A Quiet Place: Day One Opening Weekend Projections
text: A Quiet Place: Day One is anticipated to earn between $40-50 million during its opening weekend from 3,700 theaters. The prequel, which cost $67 million to produce, features Lupita Nyong'o and Joseph Quinn in a story set during the early stages of a New York City alien invasion. The original film debuted with $50 million in 2018 and eventually grossed $340 million worldwide. Its sequel opened with $48 million in May 2021 and earned nearly $300 million globally. This third installment replaces original director John Krasinski with filmmaker Michael Sarnoski, raising questions about whether audiences will engage without the principal cast members. The film is poised to challenge Disney and Pixar's Inside Out 2, which has dominated the box office for two consecutive weekends. The animated sequel earned $101 million in its second weekend and projects $55-65 million for its third frame, with over $762 million in global earnings to date. Kevin Costner's Horizon: An American Saga Chapter 1 is projected to open with only $10-15 million, despite its $100 million budget and Costner's reported $38 million personal investment in the two-film western series.
